plan: 1. Create a card lookup helper `findCardByNumber(cards: Card[], cardNumber: number): Card | undefined` in `src/domain/findCard.ts` — pure function for resolving pairsWith references to Card objects.

2. Add a pairing section to `src/ui/CardVisual/CardVisual.tsx`: accept an optional `onPairClick?: (cardNumber: number) => void` prop and the paired Card object (`pairedCard?: Card`). When `card.pairsWith` exists and `pairedCard` is resolved, render a 'Pairs with' link showing the paired card's prompt. When `pairsWith` is defined but the paired card is not found, render nothing (graceful failure). When `pairsWith` is undefined, render no pairing section at all.

3. Add CSS for the pairing section in `src/ui/CardVisual/CardVisual.module.css` — a subtle link-style element at the bottom of the card, styled to feel like a natural extension (not a button). Use the paired card's tier color for the link accent.

4. Update `src/features/browse/BrowseAllCards.tsx` to: (a) build a Map<number, Card> lookup from cardDeck for O(1) paired card resolution, (b) pass `pairedCard` and `onPairClick` props to each CardVisual, (c) implement `onPairClick` to scroll the target card into view within the browse grid (using a ref map or `document.querySelector('[data-card-number="N"]']`).
5. Add `data-card-number={card.cardNumber}` attribute to the CardVisual article element for scroll-to targeting.

6. Update play mode components (`SingleDraw.tsx`, `DrawThreeKeepOne.tsx`) to also resolve and display pairing references on drawn cards, with `onPairClick` navigating to `#/browse` and scrolling to the paired card (or simply showing the paired card inline).
7. Write unit tests in `tests/unit/find-card.test.ts`: test `findCardByNumber` returns correct card, returns undefined for missing card number, and handles empty array.

8. Write component-level tests or Playwright e2e tests verifying: (a) paired card link renders when pairsWith is present, (b) no pairing section renders when pairsWith is absent, (c) clicking a pair link scrolls to / highlights the paired card in browse view, (d) invalid pairsWith reference (card removed from deck) shows no pairing section.
9. Run `npm run lint`, `npm run typecheck`, `npm run test` to verify all checks pass.
testing_recommendations: Unit test `findCardByNumber` for: found card, missing card number, empty deck array
Unit test CardVisual rendering: pairing section shown when pairedCard provided, hidden when pairsWith undefined, hidden when pairsWith defined but pairedCard is undefined (graceful failure)
Integration test in BrowseAllCards: clicking a pair link scrolls to the target card
Playwright e2e: browse page shows 'Pairs with' on cards that have pairings (e.g. card 6), clicking navigates to card 7
Playwright e2e: cards without pairings (e.g. card 1) show no pairing section
Run full suite: npm run lint && npm run typecheck && npm run test && npm run test:e2e
risks: Scroll-to-card behavior depends on the paired card being visible after filters — if the user has tier/text filters active, the paired card may be filtered out. Mitigation: clear filters before scrolling, or show a toast explaining the card is filtered out.

Some cards pair with multiple cards (e.g. card 40 pairs with '42, 43, 44') but `pairsWith` is typed as `number | undefined` — only a single pairing is supported. Need to verify CSV data: if multi-pair values exist, the parser may need updating to `pairsWith?: number[]`. Check CSV column values carefully.
Play mode pairing navigation (step 6) adds cross-feature coupling — if scope is tight, defer play-mode pairing display to a follow-up and focus on browse mode only.
Adding props to CardVisual changes its interface, which affects all call sites (browse, SingleDraw, DrawThreeKeepOne). Ensure optional props don't break existing usage.
